system_principle: The system operates as a lunar field in which 25 tanka function as perceptual anchors connecting 1500 years of Japanese history through the emotion of absence. The moon is the invariant connector; Ma is the structural excess that prevents closure.

primary_loops.label: luna_connessione_universale
primary_loops.propagation_logic: The moon functions as a non-local connector: all separated subjects are simultaneously co-present under the same lunar field.
primary_loops.description: All absences (husband, wife, dead, departing soldier) are unified through a single invariant perceptual field.

primary_loops.label: lutto_cura_impossibile
primary_loops.propagation_logic: Mourning is encoded as continuous care toward an absent body that cannot receive it.
primary_loops.description: The system does not resolve mourning; it stabilizes it as a permanent open field.

primary_loops.label: tanka_compressione_storica
primary_loops.propagation_logic: 31 syllables function as a compression engine for historical simultaneity.
primary_loops.description: Historical figures are not sequenced but superposed within a single perceptual density.

primary_loops.label: calligrafia_campo_visivo
primary_loops.propagation_logic: Calligraphy operates as a parallel perceptual system preceding semantic reading.
primary_loops.description: The brushstroke is treated as embodied time rather than representation.

primary_loops.label: natura_emozione_stagionale
primary_loops.propagation_logic: Natural cycles encode emotional continuity rather than environmental description.
primary_loops.description: Seasonality functions as persistence of affect across absence.

global_state.system_behavior: The 25 tanka operate as a distributed perceptual network in which lunar invariance binds all separations into a single co-present field of absence.

catalog_connection.demone_samurai: narrative historical compression vs poetic compression
catalog_connection.la_singolarita_giappone: philosophical singularity vs poetic lunar singularity
